x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces DHS's open reference data (daily shelter census, drop-in centers, shelter facilities and repair scorecard, DHS contacts/intake centers, aggregate street-homeless counts) and adds request_outreach — the net-new write workflow that today exists only as a NYC311 'Homeless Person Assistance' call.

server.name: io.nyc.dhs
server.title: NYC Department of Homeless Services (DHS)
server.description: Agent-native access to the New York City Department of Homeless Services — the daily shelter census, homeless drop-in centers, shelter facilities and repair scorecard, DHS contacts and intake centers, and aggregate street-homeless counts from NYC Open Data, plus the net-new ability to request street outreach for a person experiencing unsheltered homelessness (which today lives only in a NYC311 phone call).
server.instructions: Use find_shelter_census / get_shelter_census for the daily count of people in the NYC shelter system (the flagship DHS Daily Report) by date range or a specific date. find_drop_in_centers and find_contacts locate 24/7 drop-in centers and DHS offices / intake centers (including where to apply for shelter). find_facilities covers shelter buildings, capacity, and the Shelter Repair Scorecard (open housing-maintenance violations by priority and inspecting agency). find_street_homeless_counts returns AGGREGATE unsheltered counts only — never individual people. request_outreach (net-new) reports a person sleeping on the street so a mobile outreach team responds; it mutates state — confirm the location and observed condition with the user before submitting, set emergency=true for life-safety situations (unresponsive, medical, extreme cold/heat) and remind the user those should also go to 911.

tools.name: find_shelter_census
tools.title: Find Shelter Census
tools.description: List / search the daily DHS shelter census (total individuals, single adults, families with children, adult families) by date range.

tools.name: get_shelter_census
tools.title: Get Shelter Census
tools.description: Fetch the DHS shelter census for a specific date.

tools.name: find_drop_in_centers
tools.title: Find Drop-In Centers
tools.description: List NYC homeless drop-in centers by borough or free text.

tools.name: find_facilities
tools.title: Find Facilities
tools.description: List DHS shelter facilities, capacity, and repair-scorecard condition by borough, facility type, or reporting month.

tools.name: get_facility
tools.title: Get Facility
tools.description: Fetch a DHS shelter facility (capacity, landlord, violations, census) by DHS Building ID.

tools.name: find_contacts
tools.title: Find Contacts
tools.description: List DHS offices, intake centers, and public contact points (including where to apply for shelter) by borough or free text.

tools.name: find_street_homeless_counts
tools.title: Find Street Homeless Counts
tools.description: Return AGGREGATE unsheltered / street-homeless counts by borough and period. Never returns individual people.

tools.name: list_my_outreach_requests
tools.title: List My Outreach Requests
tools.description: List the caller's own submitted street-outreach requests and their status — today trackable only as a NYC311 service request.

tools.name: request_outreach
tools.title: Request Street Outreach
tools.description: Report a person experiencing unsheltered homelessness so a DHS mobile outreach team responds. NET-NEW workflow with no public API today — it lives only in a NYC311 'Homeless Person Assistance' call. Mutates state — confirm the location and observed condition first; set emergency=true for life-safety situations (unresponsive, medical, extreme cold/heat) and remind the user those should also go to 911.
